data.title: Quantoz gains Visa nod to issue stablecoin-linked debit cards in Europe
data.description:
The Dutch electronic money issuer will act as a BIN sponsor for fintech partners, linking regulated dollar- and euro-denominated tokens to mainstream card rails.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/quantoz-gains-visa-principal-membership-to-issue-stablecoin-linked-debit-cards-in-europe?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Palo Alto Networks Shares Decline Following Earnings Forecast
data.description: Palo Alto Networks experienced a decline in its share value, dropping over 5% in extended trading. Bloomberg posted on X that the cybersecurity firm's forecast for adjusted earnings fell short of market expectations. The company's financial outlook has raised concerns among investors, leading to the decrease in stock price.

data.title: Fed's Daly Observes Buyer’s Market in Labor Sector
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve's Daly has noted that businesses are experiencing a buyer's market in the labor sector. According to ChainCatcher, this shift has led to a cautious optimism among American companies regarding demand uncertainty.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Adjusts Portfolio with Strategic Moves in Bank of America, Apple, and New York Times
data.description: Berkshire Hathaway has made significant changes to its investment portfolio, selling shares in Bank of America and Apple while acquiring a new position in the New York Times. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that these adjustments occurred during Warren Buffett’s final months as chief executive. The decision to reduce holdings in Bank of America and Apple marks a notable shift in strategy, as both companies have been long-standing investments for Berkshire Hathaway. Meanwhile, the addition of the New York Times to the portfolio suggests a strategic interest in the media sector. These moves reflect Berkshire Hathaway's ongoing efforts to optimize its investment strategy amid changing market conditions.

data.title: AI Disruption in SaaS Industry Impacts Crypto Markets
data.description:
The rise of AI agents, including Anthropic's Claude Cowork, has significantly disrupted the software-as-a-service (SaaS) industry, leading to a loss of nearly $300 billion in market value. According to NS3.AI, this transition from SaaS to AI-as-a-service has resulted in a widespread sell-off of software stocks. Consequently, capital is being redirected towards AI infrastructure providers such as Nvidia and Microsoft.

This shift in investment focus is indirectly affecting the cryptocurrency markets. As investor risk appetite changes, capital is moving away from high-growth sectors like cryptocurrency and towards AI-related investments. This trend highlights the growing influence of AI on various market sectors, including crypto.

data.title: MYX Finance Faces Selling Pressure Amid Bitcoin Consolidation
data.description: MYX Finance has recently encountered significant selling pressure, marking its first entry into oversold territory. According to NS3.AI, the token's price is mirroring Bitcoin's consolidation phase without showing signs of recovery, indicating potential further downside risks. Technical indicators and market sentiment suggest that MYX Finance may retest the $1.22 level before any meaningful rebound can be expected.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Market Stabilizes After Holiday
data.description:
The U.S. stock market reopened after a holiday, closing with stable results. According to RTHK, the Dow Jones Industrial Average initially dropped over 330 points but rebounded from its low, rising over 230 points at one point, and closed up 32 points at 49,533 points. The S&P 500 Index fell about 0.9% during the session but ended with a 0.1% increase, closing at 6,843 points, up 7 points. The Nasdaq Composite Index initially dropped more than 1% but followed the broader market's rebound, closing at 22,578 points, up 31 points, or 0.14%.

Large technology stocks showed mixed performance, with Amazon rising over 1%, Tesla falling nearly 2%, and Meta closing nearly flat. Some financial stocks performed well, with Citigroup rising nearly 3% and JPMorgan Chase increasing by almost 2%.

data.title: BlackRock Begins Seeding ETH Staking ETF
data.description:
The TradFi asset management giant is currently aping ETH to seed its latest investment product.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/news/blackrock-begins-seeding-eth-staking-etf

data.title: Meta to Expand Use of Nvidia Processors in AI Collaboration
data.description: Meta has committed to utilizing millions of Nvidia processors in the coming years, strengthening the partnership between the two leading companies in the artificial intelligence sector. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this collaboration in advancing AI technologies. This agreement underscores the growing demand for advanced processing capabilities as Meta continues to expand its AI initiatives. Nvidia's processors are expected to play a crucial role in supporting Meta's efforts to enhance its AI-driven services and products. The collaboration reflects the ongoing trend of major tech companies investing heavily in AI infrastructure to maintain competitive advantages in the rapidly evolving digital landscape.

data.title: National Australia Bank Reports Profit Increase in First Quarter
data.description: National Australia Bank (NAB) has reported a rise in its first-quarter profit, driven by growth in business banking and robust performance in home lending. Bloomberg posted on X that the bank's financial results reflect a positive trend in its core operations, highlighting the strength of its lending activities. NAB's business banking sector showed significant growth, contributing to the overall profit increase. The bank's home lending division also played a crucial role in boosting its financial performance during the quarter. This development underscores NAB's strategic focus on expanding its lending capabilities and enhancing its market position.

data.title: Eric Trump Expands Family's Defense Investments with Drone Company
data.description: Eric Trump is supporting a drone company, further diversifying his family's defense investments. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as U.S. President Donald Trump's administration continues to strengthen the defense sector. The involvement in the drone industry aligns with the administration's focus on enhancing military capabilities and technological advancements. This strategic investment reflects the family's ongoing interest in defense-related ventures, coinciding with the government's efforts to bolster the industry.

data.title: White House to Discuss Stablecoin Yields with Industry Representatives
data.description: The White House is reportedly planning to reconvene a meeting on February 19th to discuss stablecoin yields with representatives from the banking and crypto industries. According to NS3.AI, this initiative aims to address concerns and regulatory considerations surrounding stablecoin yield products. The engagement reflects ongoing governmental interest in stablecoins amidst evolving financial and regulatory landscapes.

data.title: Elemental Royalty Introduces Cryptocurrency-Backed Dividends with Tether's Tokenized Gold
data.description: Elemental Royalty has unveiled a groundbreaking initiative to distribute dividends to investors using Tether's tokenized gold. According to NS3.AI, this innovative approach marks a significant step in merging traditional finance dividends with stable, gold-backed cryptocurrency assets. The company asserts its position as the first to offer such a crypto dividend payment, highlighting its pioneering role in the financial sector.

data.title: Ethereum holds course as Buterin rejects value consensus
data.description:
Buterin says Ethereum needs no value consensus, backing permissionless blockchain, credible neutrality, Ethereum governance and as neutral infrastructure.
source: https://coincu.com/news/ethereum-holds-course-as-buterin-rejects-value-consensus/

data.title: Multiple Vehicle Collision in Colorado Results in Four Fatalities
data.description: A multi-vehicle collision in Colorado has resulted in at least four deaths. According to RTHK, the incident occurred on Tuesday morning on an interstate highway approximately 180 kilometers from Denver, the state capital. The crash involved at least 30 vehicles, leading to the confirmation of four fatalities by rescue personnel at the scene. Additionally, over a dozen individuals sustained injuries and were transported to hospitals for treatment. Authorities are currently investigating the cause of the accident, which has led to a disruption in traffic on the affected highway.

data.title: Blue Origin Aims to Beat SpaceX to the Moon
data.description: Blue Origin, the space company founded by Jeff Bezos, is determined to reach the moon ahead of its competitor, SpaceX. Bloomberg posted on X that Chief Executive Officer Dave Limp emphasized the company's commitment to achieving this goal. Blue Origin is focusing its efforts on lunar exploration as part of its broader space ambitions. The company is competing with SpaceX, led by Elon Musk, in the race to establish a presence on the moon. Both companies are working on developing technologies and strategies to support lunar missions. Blue Origin's determination to surpass SpaceX highlights the competitive nature of the private space industry, where innovation and speed are crucial to success.

data.title: Jack Altman Joins Benchmark as General Partner
data.description: Venture capitalist Jack Altman has been appointed as a general partner at Benchmark, a prominent venture capital firm. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move comes two years after Altman transitioned to full-time investing and established his own firm, Alt Capital. Altman's addition to Benchmark is seen as a strategic move to bolster the firm's investment capabilities.

data.title: Benchmarking U.S. Private Firms Using Standardized Financials
data.description: Over 1.5 million U.S. private firms generate more than $1 million in annual revenue, contributing significantly to the economy and attracting increasing investment. FactSet posted on X that benchmarking these companies' financial performance has been difficult due to limited standardized disclosure. Jennifer Hanscomb introduces a scorecard workflow utilizing tax-imputed, standardized financials to compare 10,000 sponsor-backed U.S. private companies across five key metrics: revenue, EBITDA, EBITDA margin, return on assets, and revenue per employee. This approach, supported by a unique dataset, enhances clarity in sector comparisons and offers actionable benchmarks for revenue scale, cash flow, capital efficiency, and labor productivity.

data.title: Bitdeer Surpasses MARA in Self-Mining Hash Rate
data.description: Bitdeer has achieved a significant milestone by surpassing MARA in self-mining hash rate, reaching 63.2 EH/s compared to MARA's 60.4 EH/s. According to NS3.AI, Bitdeer's growth has been propelled by its proprietary SEALMINER rigs, resulting in a remarkable 430% year-over-year increase in Bitcoin mined in January. In contrast, MARA is redirecting its focus toward artificial intelligence and has ceased reporting company-wide Bitcoin production.
